claims.evidence_text: the transpiration started to decrease in both segmented transpiration subgroups at similar VPD break points (STR+: 1.063 and STR-: 1.072 kPa)
claims.claim_summary: In segmented-transpiration durum wheat lines, whole-plant transpiration begins to be limited (breakpoint) at a VPD of roughly 1.06-1.07 kPa.

claims.evidence_text: limited (or segmented) whole-plant transpiration under high atmospheric VPD has resulted in advantageous water conservation and crop yield increase under the particular Mediterranean conditions
claims.claim_summary: Limiting whole-plant transpiration at high VPD improves water conservation and transpiration efficiency, benefiting yield under water-limited Mediterranean conditions.

claims.evidence_text: LTR lines generally exhibited better water status (stomatal conductance) and larger green biomass... The LTR lines also exhibited slightly higher gs than the STR lines
claims.claim_summary: Linear-transpiration durum wheat lines maintain higher flag-leaf stomatal conductance (gs) than segmented lines, associated with higher yield under good conditions; gs increased as growing conditions improved.

claims.evidence_text: water scarcity that usually develops during spring, which in the case of durum wheat (and other small grain cereals) coincides with the grain filling period
claims.claim_summary: Durum wheat is sensitive to terminal drought during the grain-filling period, when Mediterranean water scarcity typically develops.

claims.evidence_text: Each plot consisted of six rows 7m long and 0.2m apart, with a planting density of 250 seeds m-2.
claims.claim_summary: Durum wheat field trials were sown at a planting density of 250 seeds per square metre.

claims.evidence_text: Each plot consisted of six rows 7m long and 0.2m apart, with a planting density of 250 seeds m-2.
claims.claim_summary: Durum wheat was sown in rows spaced 0.2 m (20 cm) apart.
Across Spanish durum wheat field trials, growing-season rainfall ranged about 162-258 mm, with supplemental irrigation of 125-180 mm added in irrigated treatments.

claims.evidence_text: the fertilization was applied in two steps, a first basal application and then a second top dressing application... Prior sowing 8:15:15 NPK ... Top dressing Calcium ammonium nitrate / Diluted urea (46%)
claims.claim_summary: Wheat nitrogen fertilization was split into two applications: a basal NPK application prior to sowing and a later top dressing (calcium ammonium nitrate or diluted urea).

claims.evidence_text: the N content in grains, which is an indicator of the capacity of the grains to accumulate nitrogen, decreased as growing conditions improved
claims.claim_summary: Grain nitrogen content (proxy for grain protein) in durum wheat decreased as growing/yield conditions improved (dilution effect).

claims.evidence_text: LTR lines exhibit higher grain yield, biomass and greenness under good to optimal agronomical conditions, whereas the STR lines perform better under the most water-limiting trials
claims.claim_summary: Linear-transpiration durum wheat lines accumulate higher biomass and green canopy (NDVI) under good conditions, while segmented lines accumulate more under severe water stress.
